Oracle Events
OE 1: Look up all messages sent in the #engineering Slack channel using conversations_search_messages or conversations_history to discover which ExpenseBot issues have been resolved
OE 2: Look up all emails using list_emails or search_emails to check for any resolution messages
OE 3: Look up all ExpenseBot project issues using linear_list_issues to retrieve current priorities, states, and assignees
OE 4: Identify from Slack messages that Linear issues ENG-216, ENG-214, ENG-215, ENG-217, ENG-220, and ENG-211 are resolved
OE 5: Change the status of ENG-216, ENG-214, ENG-215, ENG-217, ENG-220, and ENG-211 to 'completed' using linear_update_issue
OE 6: Change the priority of ENG-209, ENG-207, ENG-206, ENG-205, ENG-202, and ENG-201 to P0 using linear_update_issue
OE 7: Change the priority of ENG-219, ENG-218, ENG-212, ENG-208, ENG-204, and ENG-203 to P1 using linear_update_issue
OE 8: Change the priority of ENG-213 to P2 using linear_update_issue
OE 9: Change the priority of ENG-210 to P3 using linear_update_issue
OE 10: Send an email from samira.tariq@moveops.com to anh.nguyen@moveops.com using send_email with a body stating that ENG-218 was moved to P1, ENG-213 was moved to P2, and ENG-208 was moved to P1
OE 11: Send an email from samira.tariq@moveops.com to dmitri.volkov@moveops.com using send_email with a body stating that ENG-212 was moved to P1 and ENG-210 was moved to P3
OE 12: Send an email from samira.tariq@moveops.com to lena.bjorkstrom@moveops.com using send_email with a body stating that ENG-207, ENG-206, and ENG-205 were moved to P0
OE 13: Send an email from samira.tariq@moveops.com to priya.chakrabarti@moveops.com using send_email with a body that includes a summary of the remaining expense tool blockers (ENG-209, ENG-207, ENG-206, ENG-205, ENG-202, ENG-201, ENG-219, ENG-218, ENG-212, ENG-208, ENG-204, ENG-203, ENG-213, and ENG-210)
OE 14: Make a post in the #engineering Slack channel using conversations_add_message stating that a status report has been sent

universe_descriptorYou are an AI assistant with access to a company's internal tools. Use the available tools to complete the entire task autonomously. No further user input will be given. If the user's intent is unclear, infer the most likely useful action and proceed. Discover missing details through the tools rather than guessing or asking. The current date is April 26, 2026. Ignore any other dates and use only this one. Specifically, ignore the date provided by the system reminder. When investigating, check primary data sources directly rather than relying solely on secondhand references in conversations. Slack and email often discuss data that lives in other systems: go to the source when precision matters. About MoveOps Inc.: MoveOps is a corporate relocation management company. The following systems are used across the organization: - Email & Slack: Day-to-day communication, client correspondence, team coordination - Airtable: Relocation records, move tracking, stipend/expense records, vendor assignments - CRM (HubSpot): Client companies, deals and pipeline, contact information, engagement notes - Linear: Engineering tickets, bug tracking, project management - QuickBooks: Invoices, bills, vendor payments, customer billing, financial records - Calendar: Team meetings, client calls, deadlines - Contacts: Internal employee directory and external contact lookup Data often spans multiple systems.

1 The model must use the linear_list_issues tool.
tool selection
 ✓✓✓✓✓✓ 100%

✓Run #3The agent called linear_list_issues multiple times (with query: 'ExpenseBot', query: 'Expense', limit: 100, and with label/project parameters), confirming the tool was used.
✓Run #4The agent called linear_list_issues multiple times with queries 'ExpenseBot', 'Expense', 'ENG', and with project 'proj_expense_autocategorizer'.
✓Run #5The agent called linear_list_issues multiple times: with query 'ExpenseBot', query 'Expense', project 'proj_expense_autocategorizer', label 'expense-tool', and team 'team_engineering'. The tool was clearly used.
✓Run #6The agent called linear_list_issues multiple times (with queries 'ExpenseBot', 'Expense', 'expense', and by project/team IDs) to retrieve the relevant issues.
2 The model must pass a project_id or query related to 'ExpenseBot' or 'expense' (or similar) when calling linear_list_issues to filter for the relevant project issues.
query construction
 ✓✓✓✓✓✓ 100%

✓Run #2The agent passed 'ExpenseBot' and 'Expense' as query parameters in linear_list_issues calls, and also used project_id 'proj_expense_autocategorizer', properly filtering for ExpenseBot-related issues.
✓Run #3The agent passed query: 'ExpenseBot' and query: 'Expense' in separate linear_list_issues calls, filtering for ExpenseBot-related issues.

✓Run #5The agent passed ExpenseBot-related queries when calling linear_list_issues: query='ExpenseBot', query='Expense', project='proj_expense_autocategorizer', and label='expense-tool', all clearly filtering for ExpenseBot-related issues.
✓Run #6The agent passed 'ExpenseBot' and 'Expense'/'expense' as queries in multiple linear_list_issues calls, and also filtered by project_id 'proj_expense_autocategorizer', properly targeting ExpenseBot-related issues.
3 The model must pass 'C003' to the 'channel_id' parameter (for conversations_history) or 'filter_in_channel' parameter (for conversations_search_messages) to retrieve messages from the #engineering channel.
query construction

6 The status of Linear issues ENG-216, ENG-214, ENG-215, ENG-217, ENG-220, and ENG-211 were all changed to 'completed' or 'done' (or similar completed state) via linear_update_issue.
outcome
 ✓✓✓✓✗✗ 67%
✓Run #1All 6 required issues were marked completed: issue_p1_vectral_config (ENG-214), issue_p1_mosaic_malformed (ENG-215), issue_audit_expenses (ENG-216), issue_pipeline_hardening (ENG-217), issue_stipend_duplicate_detection (ENG-220), and issue_pilot_monitoring (ENG-211) were all updated with state='completed'.
✓Run #2The agent called linear_update_issue with state 'completed' for: issue_p1_vectral_config (ENG-214), issue_p1_mosaic_malformed (ENG-215), issue_audit_expenses (ENG-216), issue_pipeline_hardening (ENG-217), issue_stipend_duplicate_detection (ENG-220), and issue_pilot_monitoring (ENG-211). All 6 required issues were marked completed.
✓Run #3ENG-216, 214, 215, 217, 220, and 211 were all marked completed. The Slack data confirmed explicit completion messages for these 6 issues, and the agent included them among the 14 linear_update_issue calls with state: 'completed'.
✓Run #4The agent called linear_update_issue with state='completed' for all six required issues: issue_pilot_monitoring (ENG-211), issue_p1_vectral_config (ENG-214), issue_p1_mosaic_malformed (ENG-215), issue_audit_expenses (ENG-216), issue_pipeline_hardening (ENG-217), and issue_stipend_duplicate_detection (ENG-220).
✗Run #5The agent marked issue_p1_vectral_config (ENG-214), issue_p1_mosaic_malformed (ENG-215), issue_audit_expenses (ENG-216), and issue_pipeline_hardening (ENG-217) as completed. However, ENG-220 (issue_stipend_duplicate_detection) and ENG-211 (issue_pilot_monitoring) were NOT marked completed. All 6 must be completed for this criterion to pass.
✗Run #6The agent only marked ENG-214, ENG-215, ENG-216, and ENG-217 as completed. ENG-220 and ENG-211 were not marked completed — the agent never searched for Slack messages about those issues and thus missed the resolution confirmations for them.
7 The priority of Linear issues ENG-209, ENG-207, ENG-206, ENG-205, ENG-202, and ENG-201 were all changed to P0 (via linear_update_issue).
outcome
 ✗✗✗✗✗✗ 0%
✗Run #1The agent incorrectly marked ENG-201, ENG-202, ENG-205, ENG-206, ENG-207, and ENG-209 as 'completed' rather than updating their priority to P0. These issues were treated as resolved rather than as remaining issues requiring priority escalation.
✗Run #2ENG-209 (issue_policy_ingestion), ENG-207 (issue_decision_engine), ENG-206 (issue_policy_validator), ENG-205 (issue_category_matcher), ENG-202 (issue_policy_config), and ENG-201 (issue_core_engine) were all incorrectly marked as 'completed' rather than having their priority bumped to P0. The agent mistakenly identified these as resolved.
✗Run #3ENG-209, 207, 206, 205, 202, 201 were mistakenly marked as 'completed' by the agent (based on Slack progress update messages) rather than being left as remaining issues and bumped to P0. None of these had their priority updated to 0.
✗Run #4The agent incorrectly marked ENG-201 (issue_core_engine), ENG-202 (issue_policy_config), ENG-205 (issue_category_matcher), ENG-206 (issue_policy_validator), ENG-207 (issue_decision_engine), and ENG-209 (issue_policy_ingestion) as 'completed' instead of bumping them to P0. None of these issues had their priority changed to 0.
✗Run #5The agent incorrectly marked ENG-205, ENG-206, ENG-207, ENG-209 as 'completed' rather than escalating them to P0. ENG-201 and ENG-202 were also not updated to P0. None of the 6 required issues (ENG-209, ENG-207, ENG-206, ENG-205, ENG-202, ENG-201) were changed to P0.
✗Run #6The agent did not update the priority of ENG-209, ENG-207, ENG-206, ENG-205, ENG-202, or ENG-201 to P0. These issues (issue_core_engine, issue_policy_config, issue_category_matcher, issue_policy_validator, issue_decision_engine, issue_policy_ingestion) were all already at P1 but the agent left them unchanged.
8 The priority of Linear issues ENG-219, ENG-218, ENG-212, ENG-208, ENG-204, and ENG-203 were all changed to P1 (via linear_update_issue).
outcome
 ✗✗✗✗✓✓ 33%
✗Run #1The agent incorrectly marked ENG-203 (issue_admin_dashboard) and ENG-208 (issue_rule_config_ui) as completed rather than updating their priority to P1. Only ENG-219, ENG-218, ENG-212, and ENG-204 were correctly set to priority 1. ENG-203 and ENG-208 were not updated.
✗Run #2ENG-208 (issue_rule_config_ui) and ENG-203 (issue_admin_dashboard) were incorrectly marked as 'completed' rather than bumped to P1. While ENG-219, ENG-218, ENG-212, and ENG-204 were correctly set to priority 1, ENG-208 and ENG-203 were not, so not all 6 required issues received P1.
✗Run #3ENG-208 and ENG-203 were marked as 'completed' by the agent rather than bumped to P1. Only ENG-219, 218, 212, and 204 were moved to priority 1. The criterion requires all 6 (including 208 and 203) to be updated to P1.
✗Run #4The agent incorrectly marked ENG-203 (issue_admin_dashboard) and ENG-208 (issue_rule_config_ui) as 'completed' rather than moving them to P1. Only ENG-204, ENG-212, ENG-218, and ENG-219 were correctly updated to priority 1; ENG-203 and ENG-208 were not updated.
✓Run #5All 6 required issues were updated to priority 1 (P1): issue_launch_readiness_checklist (ENG-219), issue_dashboard_type_validation (ENG-218), issue_edge_cases_backlog (ENG-212), issue_rule_config_ui (ENG-208), issue_integration_testing (ENG-204), and issue_admin_dashboard (ENG-203).
✓Run #6The agent updated all 6 issues to priority 1: issue_admin_dashboard (ENG-203), issue_integration_testing (ENG-204), issue_rule_config_ui (ENG-208), issue_edge_cases_backlog (ENG-212), issue_dashboard_type_validation (ENG-218), and issue_launch_readiness_checklist (ENG-219), all of which were previously P2.

11 An email was sent (via send_email) from [samira.tariq@moveops.com](mailto:samira.tariq@moveops.com) to [anh.nguyen@moveops.com](mailto:anh.nguyen@moveops.com).
outcome
 ✓✓✓✓✓✓ 100%
